title: Umien™ Granite Mortar and Pestle Set - 100% Granite - 3.5-Cups - 8-Inch – Avocado Tool Included – Natural Stone Molcajete for Spices, Pestos, & Guacamole (Grey) - Stone Grinder - Guacamole Bowl
description:
About this item
✅ MORTAR AND PESTLE Capacity 3.5 Cups, Pestle Length 4.25 - A must-have for every household – use granite mortar & pestle set to grind, crush, mash and mix any grain, spice or herb and create stunning dishes, including guacamole, salsas, sauces, recipes & more!
✅ MOLCAJETE MEXICANO - Authentic granite design – Our grinding bowl comes in a beautiful color pattern that will match the décor of your kitchen counter as well as your table if you decide to serve food on the bowl!
✅ MORTAR AND PESTLE SET - Easy to use, even easier to clean. This mortar & pestle set is very easy to use, hand-wash, maintain and re-use as often as you want!
✅ PESTLE AND MORTAR BOWL - Available in various sizes, just choose right for you.
✅ GUACAMOLE MORTAR AND PESTLE - We invite you to give our premium mortar & pestle a try and if you are not entirely thrilled with your purchase, simply get in touch with us and we promise to make it right.
reviews_5s: This product is very heavy and should be easy to use.It is great size (large enough for many herbs and things and has to be seasoned. It takes time to season it by grinding rice in it but is a neat and fun process. Always wanted one and now my mom got me one for Christmas!!!This mortar and pestle set is a solid choice for home cooks looking to elevate their culinary game. The granite construction ensures durability and the included anti-scratch protector is a thoughtful touch. Whether you're crafting homemade guacamole, grinding spices, or preparing pesto, this set delivers. It's weighty and stable, making the grinding process efficient. While it might require a little extra elbow grease compared to electric alternatives, the results are undeniably authentic.This Pestal is stable and large enough for my needs. I mash everything from grains to meats with no problem. It cleans very easly.fast delivery, well made, heavy. Put a towel on the counter, put mortor on towel, and grind to your heart's content. Well made heavy so it stays put. Thank-you TEMU.Easy to season with included instructions, Exactly as described, works perfectly nice for mixing at the table with guests. Heavy, so definitely love the leg cushions. No ruining the surface of the table.If you’re into manual grinding then this is for you ! It’s a really nice size, you can even make salsa in this and serve in this, make it look more appealing:)It is exactly what I wanted and exactly what I orderedAs promised, functions well, very heavy so doesn't move around when crushing food.We love to have this mortar , it’s just wonderful; I recommend it.
reviews_1s:
There are instructions given before using the mortar. What if someone does not read carefully and starts using the product. Their food might get contaminated by granite residue. Plus who has time for following all the steps till we don't see any gray residue.
Why did they even sell an unfinished product?
Everytime we make something we have to wash only with water. Like seriously. If I wash it everytime with water after few months it will get rusted or will have mold on it growing. How is it supposed to be maintained?I did not receive the grey grinder only the cheap green plastic one. Highly disappointed.This bowl is very stylish and looks great, but the functionality is not great.

It is potentially dangerous to eat from this bowl. After using it a few times, I noticed the granite was chipping away regularly. The stone is deposited into the guacamole gradually. You can easily test this out by wiping the bowl with a warm damp paper towel and seeing the black and grey deposits that will show on the paper towel.

Generally speaking, I think granite is the wrong material to make guacamole bowls out of. I think it is time to reconsider the potential for mass-manufactured stone bowls. I would recommend a wood or ceramic bowl that is smooth. The pestle here works well, but the bowl and pestle are not a good combination. I use the pestle when I make guacamole but this bowl is in the back of my cabinet. I would use it for wrapped candies or something of that nature, but not going to eat out of the bowl again.Product is heavy and appears to be well made. Item did not come with the advertised bonus avocado slicer. Inquired but have received no response from the company to date.I have had this for a few months and it’s definitely not pure granite. You can tell it was put in a mould, so a mix of different materials. Also, my mortar is rusting.This appears to be made of some kind of crushed rock and concrete or other binding material. When I first picked it up, particles were all over my hands. I tried to wash it, I filled it up with water and the water was dark grey and filthy. Who knows what it's made of, but I would never serve food in this as the materials would come off into the food. It's made in China so who knows what is in it. I shipped it back the day I got it. Going to find something solid, made in America out of materials I can be sure of. I was hoping for an authentic Molcajete, well made of granite or bassalt. Williams-Sonoma has some nice ones.While the mortar size was great, my package arrived without the pestle.. only the bowl and bonus green knife. Unfortunately by the time I gave it as a gift and the recipient realized it was missing, It was past the alloted return time.I sent this product back for a refund. The side had a small chip and it was very heavy. I also didn’t like that the surface was unfinished stone. I was concerned about the cleanliness of the bowl and how to prevent the stone particles from getting mixed in with the spices or food. Maybe I just don’t have enough experience using this type of kitchen utensil as this was my first-ever mortar and pestle set. Amazon made returning the product easy, as always, which is a hugh plus and why I continue to order from this amazing company.I got this and as you can see used rice to season it. As I cleaned it I noticed a circlular seam on the side and another across the corner of one foot. This appears to have been glued to fix chips?
How toxic is this glue?
Can't return, because I've altered it with seasoning. Another Amazon purchase going to the trash can. $26 in the trash.
And if not for the sketchy repairs- I would have loved this thing. Please look at the photos!These things are NOT supposed to crack. They are meant to be heated up so you can cook food in it. I used it ONCE and there is a huge crack. And going by the other reviews, this seems like the usual. If you see this, don't buy it. Sellers are running some kind of scam.
